$comment: DISCLAIMER: This reference data is for informational purposes only. It does NOT constitute legal or tax advice. Tax laws change frequently — always verify with current legislation and qualified professionals. Data current as of March 2026.
title: Inheritance & Estate Tax Thresholds
description: Tax-free thresholds, nil rate bands, and exemption amounts for inheritance and estate taxes by jurisdiction. These determine the value below which no tax is payable.

GB.name: United Kingdom

thresholds.label: Nil Rate Band (NRB)
thresholds.description: The standard threshold below which no IHT is charged. Frozen at £325,000 until April 2028.
thresholds.transferableNote: Unused NRB can be transferred to a surviving spouse or civil partner, effectively doubling the threshold to £650,000.

thresholds.label: Residence Nil Rate Band (RNRB)
thresholds.description: Additional threshold available when a main residence is passed to direct descendants (children, grandchildren). Tapers by £1 for every £2 the estate exceeds £2,000,000.

thresholds.label: Maximum Combined Threshold (married/civil partner)
thresholds.description: Where both NRB and RNRB are transferable between spouses: £325,000 + £175,000 = £500,000 per person, £1,000,000 for a couple.
conditions: Estate value does not exceed taper threshold
Surviving spouse/civil partner transfers unused allowances

GB.source: Inheritance Tax Act 1984, ss. 7, 8A–8M; Finance (No. 2) Act 2015, s. 9

US.name: United States

thresholds.label: Federal Estate Tax Exemption
thresholds.description: The basic exclusion amount for 2024. Indexed for inflation annually. Unified with the lifetime gift tax exemption under the Tax Cuts and Jobs Act 2017. Scheduled to revert to approximately $7,000,000 (inflation-adjusted) after 31 December 2025 unless extended.
thresholds.portabilityNote: Deceased spousal unused exclusion (DSUE) can be transferred to the surviving spouse by filing Form 706 within 9 months of death.
thresholds.sunsetNote: The TCJA doubled exemption is scheduled to sunset. Without legislative action, the exemption reverts to pre-TCJA levels (approximately $7,000,000 indexed).

thresholds.label: State-Level Estate/Inheritance Tax Thresholds
thresholds.description: Several US states impose their own estate or inheritance taxes with lower thresholds than the federal exemption. These vary widely.

examples.note: No inflation indexing. Lowest state threshold.
2024 amount. Indexed for inflation. Cliff provision: if estate exceeds 105% of exemption, entire estate is taxable.

thresholds.note: Maryland is the only jurisdiction with both an estate tax and an inheritance tax.

US.source: Internal Revenue Code §§ 2001, 2010; Tax Cuts and Jobs Act 2017 (P.L. 115-97)

AU.name: Australia
AU.taxName: N/A — No inheritance or estate tax
AU.description: Australia abolished federal estate duty in 1979 and state-level death duties by 1984. There is no inheritance tax. However, capital gains tax (CGT) may apply when beneficiaries dispose of inherited assets. The deceased's cost base transfers to the beneficiary.
AU.cgtNote: Assets acquired by the deceased before 20 September 1985 receive a market value cost base as at the date of death. Assets acquired after that date retain the deceased's original cost base.

CA.name: Canada
CA.taxName: N/A — No inheritance or estate tax
CA.description: Canada has no inheritance or estate tax. However, there is a deemed disposition at fair market value on death, triggering capital gains tax. The first $250,000 of capital gains above the annual exemption is taxed at 50% inclusion; amounts above $250,000 are taxed at 66.67% inclusion (from 2024).
CA.principalResidenceNote: A principal residence is exempt from capital gains tax on deemed disposition at death.
CA.source: Income Tax Act (R.S.C., 1985, c. 1 (5th Supp.)), s. 70(5)

thresholds.label: Abattement en ligne directe
thresholds.description: Tax-free allowance per child (or parent inheriting from child). Applied per beneficiary.

thresholds.label: Exemption for surviving spouse/civil partner (PACS)
thresholds.description: Surviving spouse or PACS partner is fully exempt from succession tax since 2007.

thresholds.label: Spouse/civil partner allowance
thresholds.description: Tax-free allowance for surviving spouse or registered civil partner.

thresholds.label: Siblings, nieces, nephews, etc.
thresholds.description: Tax-free allowance for Tax Class II beneficiaries.

thresholds.label: All other beneficiaries
thresholds.description: Tax-free allowance for unrelated persons (Tax Class III).

IN.name: India
IN.taxName: N/A — No inheritance or estate tax
IN.description: India abolished estate duty in 1985 via the Estate Duty (Amendment) Act 1985. There is currently no inheritance tax, estate tax, or gift tax on inheritances. However, income earned from inherited assets is taxable as normal income.
IN.note: Gifts received from non-relatives exceeding ₹50,000 are taxable under Section 56(2)(x) of the Income Tax Act 1961, but inheritances are specifically excluded.
IN.source: Estate Duty (Amendment) Act 1985; Income Tax Act 1961, s. 56(2)(x)

thresholds.label: Basic deduction
thresholds.description: ¥30,000,000 plus ¥6,000,000 per statutory heir. For example, a spouse and two children yields a threshold of ¥48,000,000.

thresholds.label: Spouse tax credit
thresholds.description: The surviving spouse pays no inheritance tax on the greater of ¥160,000,000 or their statutory share (typically one-half).

SG.name: Singapore
SG.description: Singapore abolished estate duty on 15 February 2008. There is no inheritance tax, estate tax, or capital gains tax on inherited assets.

IE.name: Ireland

thresholds.label: Group A — Child of the disponer
thresholds.description: Lifetime threshold for gifts/inheritances from a parent to a child (including adopted children and certain foster children).

thresholds.label: Group B — Linear ancestor, descendant, sibling, niece, nephew
thresholds.description: Lifetime threshold for gifts/inheritances from close relatives.

thresholds.label: Group C — All other relationships
thresholds.description: Lifetime threshold for all other beneficiaries.

IE.note: Thresholds are cumulative over a lifetime — all taxable gifts and inheritances from persons in the same group are aggregated.
IE.source: Capital Acquisitions Tax Consolidation Act 2003, Part 2

NZ.name: New Zealand
NZ.taxName: N/A — No inheritance or estate tax
NZ.description: New Zealand abolished estate duty on 1 January 1993 and gift duty on 1 October 2011. There is no inheritance tax, estate tax, or capital gains tax (with limited exceptions for certain property transactions).
NZ.source: Estate Duty Abolition Act 1993; Taxation (Tax Administration and Remedial Matters) Act 2011
